title: Stelis cyathiflora
taxonomy: Stelis cyathiflora is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Stelis, which is a group of orchids commonly known as the 'miniature pleurothallids'. This species is native to Central and South America, specifically in countries such as Costa Rica and Panama. The classification of Stelis cyathiflora is further broken down into subcategories, including its kingdom (Plantae), phylum (Angiospermophyta), class (Liliopsida), and order (Asparagales). The specific classification of this orchid species is essential for understanding its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
uses: Stelis cyathiflora is used in traditional medicine for its potential benefits in treating various health issues. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. In some regions, it is used to treat fever, headaches, and respiratory problems. Additionally, the plant is believed to have wound-healing properties, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Further research is necessary to confirm the efficacy of Stelis cyathiflora's medicinal uses.

intro: Stelis cyathiflora, also known as the orchid Stelis cyathiflora, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Stelis cyathiflora include flavonoids and phenolic compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Stelis cyathiflora is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
